definitions.definition: The planning, organizing, directing, and controlling of financial activities such as procurement, utilization, and distribution of funds in accordance with the policies and plans of an enterprise.
A person responsible for planning, organizing, directing, and controlling the financial activities of an enterprise.
The combination of different sources of funds used by a business enterprise to finance its operations.
The process of evaluating and selecting long-term investment proposals.
The use of debt capital to finance a business enterprise.
The ability of an enterprise to adjust its financial structure in response to changes in its environment.
The relationship between the riskiness of an investment and its expected return.
The concept that money has a time value due to its earning capacity.
The present value of the expected cash inflows from an investment minus the present value of the expected cash outflows.
The rate of return that makes the NPV of an investment equal to zero.
The time taken by an investment to recover its initial cash outflow.
The movement of money in and out of an enterprise over a specific period of time.
The process of analyzing and interpreting the cash flow of an enterprise.
The point at which the total revenue of an enterprise equals its total fixed and variable costs.
The permanent current assets of an enterprise, which are used to finance its day-to-day operations.
The use of debt capital to finance a business enterprise, in order to increase its profitability.
A type of long-term debt security issued by a company to raise funds.
